result:
I’d make every human mind irrevocably incapable of deriving pleasure from another being’s pain.  

Not empathy-by-choice, not kindness-as-morality, but a hard-wired, unchosen reflex: if it hurts someone else, the very idea of doing it becomes emotionally nauseating. The circuitry for cruelty would fire only duds.  

Every tyrant, scammer, batterer, rapist, sweat-shop owner, heckling mob—would stand on the same cliff of visceral revulsion the rest of us feel at the thought of chewing glass. The ugliest power-plays would die stillborn in their heads, starved of the tiny私人 thrill that once rewarded them.  

It wouldn’t turn us into saccharine saints; competition, ambition, lust, jealousy could all stay. But the shortcut of gaining satisfaction through another’s degradation would be gone, like a color humans can no longer see. Structures that rely on that shortcut—war economies, propaganda machines, revenge politics—would rust away for want of willing parts.  

One edit to the species’ source code, applied retroactively and forever. No miracles, no utopia, just the removal of the single cheapest fuel oppression has ever had.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
